---
title: "Betting against beta"
subtitle: "a study of proposition 1 & 2 in american equity markets"
author: "Tobias Dines Schlünssen & Conrad Frederik Kromann"
date: today
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"
---
```{=latex}

% --- Info page (KU template page 2) ---
\thispagestyle{empty}
\vspace*{2cm}
\begin{tabular}{@{}p{3.5cm} p{9cm}}
  Institute:          & Department of Economics \\[1ex]
  Faculty:            & Faculty of Social Sciences (SAMF) \\[1ex]
  Author(s):          & Tobias Dines Schlünssen (GFS189)\\
                        & Conrad Frederik Kromann (RTL959)\\[1ex]
  Title and subtitle: & Betting against beta\\
                        &- A study of proposition 1 \& 2 in american equity markets \\[1ex]
  Description:        & [A short abstract or description of your thesis goes here.] \\[1ex]
  Advisor:            & Jacob Lundbeck Serup\\[1ex]
  Date:               & June 2nd, 2026 \\
\end{tabular}
\clearpage
% Abstract page
\begin{abstract}
This paper examines the market anomaly where stocks with higher betas tends to have lower 
alphas than those with lower betas. This is done by testing the proposition 1 and 2 from 
the paper: \textit{Betting against beta} by @frazzinipedersen2014 .
Using data from US equity markets we test proposition 1 by examining the 
relationship between beta and alpha to test for the presence of the market anomaly. To test 
proposition 2, we construct a BAB-portfolio to examine the performance of a strategy which 
exploits this market anomaly by going long on low beta stocks and short on high beta stocks.
We find...
\end{abstract}

\clearpage

\renewcommand{\contentsname}{Table of content}
\tableofcontents
\clearpage


```

{{< include chapters/01_introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/02_theory.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/03_data_and_methodology.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/04_results.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/05_discussion.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/06_conclusion.qmd >}}

# References
::::{#refs}
::::
